description: Production-grade requirements extraction from a detailed expense tracker product idea

parameters.idea: We want to build a mobile expense tracker app for small business owners...

functional_requirements.title: Receipt Photo Capture
functional_requirements.description: The system shall allow users to photograph receipts using the device camera with auto-crop and image quality validation

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated as core feature and confirmed as MVP priority by stakeholder guidance. 'photograph receipts using their phone camera'.

citation.source_reference: Users should be able to photograph receipts using their phone camera

trajectory.detail: Identified receipt photography as primary input mechanism
Core to value proposition — replaces paper receipts. Must-Have per stakeholder guidance.
Formulated with auto-crop implied by 'automatically extract' downstream need

functional_requirements.title: Automatic OCR Data Extraction
functional_requirements.description: The system shall automatically extract amount, date, vendor name, and payment method from receipt images using OCR with ≥90% accuracy

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated with specific fields listed. Accuracy target inferred from 'automatically' implying reliable extraction.

citation.source_reference: automatically extract the amount, date, vendor name, and payment method from the receipt using OCR

trajectory.detail: Four fields specified: amount, date, vendor, payment method
Core automation value prop — without this users still type manually
Added ≥90% accuracy as measurable target for 'automatically'

functional_requirements.title: Expense Categorisation
functional_requirements.description: The system shall categorise expenses into predefined categories (travel, meals & entertainment, office supplies, software subscriptions, professional services, vehicle/mileage, utilities) with automatic suggestion and manual override

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated with 7 predefined categories listed. Auto-categorisation implied by OCR extraction feeding into category assignment.

citation.source_reference: categorise expenses into standard categories (travel, meals & entertainment, office supplies, software subscriptions, professional services, vehicle/mileage, utilities)

trajectory.detail: 7 categories explicitly listed plus 'create custom categories'
Core feature — enables budgeting and reporting downstream
Included auto-suggestion + manual override for user control

functional_requirements.title: Custom Category Creation
functional_requirements.description: The system shall allow users to create, edit, and delete custom expense categories in addition to the predefined set

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated — 'allow users to create custom categories'. Should-Have as predefined categories cover most use cases for MVP.

citation.source_reference: allow users to create custom categories

trajectory.detail: Custom categories explicitly mentioned
Enhances categorisation but not blocking for MVP — 7 predefined categories suffice initially
Added edit/delete alongside create for complete CRUD

functional_requirements.title: Monthly and Quarterly Report Generation
functional_requirements.description: The system shall generate monthly and quarterly expense reports with category breakdowns, totals, and trend comparisons to prior periods

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated with specific cadences (monthly, quarterly) and content (breakdowns, totals, trends). Should-Have per stakeholder guidance — 'Reporting can come later'.

citation.source_reference: generate monthly and quarterly expense reports broken down by category, with totals and trends compared to prior periods

trajectory.detail: Monthly + quarterly cadence, category breakdown, trends
Stakeholder says reporting can come later — Should-Have, not Must-Have
Captured all specified report attributes

functional_requirements.title: Report Export (PDF and CSV)
functional_requirements.description: The system shall export expense reports as PDF and CSV formats suitable for submission to accountants

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated — 'export reports as PDF and CSV for their accountant'. Follows reporting priority.

citation.source_reference: export reports as PDF and CSV for their accountant

trajectory.detail: Two formats specified, accountant use case
Paired with reporting — same priority tier
Specified 'suitable for submission' quality bar

functional_requirements.title: Year-End Tax Summary
functional_requirements.description: The system shall generate a year-end tax summary report matching HMRC self-assessment expense categories

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated with specific compliance target (HMRC self-assessment). Should-Have as it's reporting tier.

citation.source_reference: generate a year-end tax summary matching HMRC self-assessment categories

trajectory.detail: HMRC self-assessment mapping explicitly required
High value for target users (freelancers) but reporting tier
Linked to regulatory compliance (HMRC)

functional_requirements.title: Budget Setting Per Category
functional_requirements.description: The system shall allow users to set monthly spending budget limits per expense category

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated — 'set spending budgets per category per month'. Must-Have as it enables the alert system.

citation.source_reference: set spending budgets per category per month

trajectory.detail: Budget + per category + per month specified
Prerequisite for alerts — Must-Have to enable downstream feature
Scoped to monthly cadence as stated

functional_requirements.title: Budget Threshold Push Notifications
functional_requirements.description: The system shall send push notifications to the user when spending reaches 80% and 100% of the configured budget for any category

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated with specific thresholds (80%, 100%). Must-Have as the primary budgeting feedback mechanism.

citation.source_reference: receive push notifications when they reach 80% and 100% of their budget

trajectory.detail: Two threshold levels explicitly specified
Push notifications = proactive value, not just passive data
Captured exact thresholds from input

functional_requirements.title: Weekly Spending Summary Notification
functional_requirements.description: The system shall send a weekly spending summary push notification every Sunday evening

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated — 'weekly spending summary notification every Sunday evening'. Could-Have as it's a nice-to-have engagement feature, not core functionality.

citation.source_reference: get a weekly spending summary notification every Sunday evening

trajectory.detail: Weekly cadence, Sunday evening timing specified
Engagement feature — app works fine without it
Could-Have — not blocking any other feature

functional_requirements.title: Email/Password Authentication
functional_requirements.description: The system shall support user registration and login via email and password

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated — 'Authentication should use email/password'. Must-Have as the app requires user accounts.

citation.source_reference: Authentication should use email/password

trajectory.detail: Email/password explicitly specified as primary auth
Foundational — everything else requires a logged-in user
Must-Have — blocks all personalised features

functional_requirements.title: Biometric Authentication
functional_requirements.description: The system shall support optional biometric login (Face ID / fingerprint) for returning users

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated as 'optional biometric'. Should-Have — enhances UX for returning users but email/password works alone.

citation.source_reference: optional biometric (Face ID / fingerprint) for returning users

trajectory.detail: Optional + Face ID + fingerprint + returning users
'Optional' = not Must-Have. Convenience layer.
Should-Have — great UX but not MVP blocking

non_functional_requirements.title: Offline Operation with Auto-Sync
non_functional_requirements.description: The system shall function fully offline for receipt capture, categorisation, and budget viewing, and automatically synchronise all data when connectivity is restored

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated with 'must' — 'The app must work fully offline'. Use case justified: 'client sites or on trains where connectivity is poor'.

citation.source_reference: The app must work fully offline — users often photograph receipts at client sites or on trains where connectivity is poor. All data should sync automatically when back online

trajectory.detail: 'must work fully offline' + auto-sync + use case justification
'must' = Must-Have. Architectural impact: local-first with sync layer.
NFR-Reliability as it's a quality attribute not a capability

non_functional_requirements.title: Multi-Device Conflict Resolution
non_functional_requirements.description: The system shall resolve data conflicts when the same receipt is edited on multiple devices, preserving the most recent edit with audit trail

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly mentioned — 'conflict resolution if the same receipt was edited on multiple devices'. Should-Have as multi-device is implied but not confirmed as MVP scope.

citation.source_reference: conflict resolution if the same receipt was edited on multiple devices

trajectory.detail: Conflict resolution explicitly mentioned
Implies multi-device usage — Should-Have for MVP, Must-Have later
Added 'most recent edit + audit trail' as standard conflict strategy

non_functional_requirements.title: User and Data Volume Capacity
non_functional_requirements.description: The system shall support 10,000 concurrent users with approximately 500,000 receipts processed per month (10,000 users × 50 receipts)

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated capacity targets — '10,000 users in the first year with approximately 50 receipts per user per month'.

citation.source_reference: handle up to 10,000 users in the first year with approximately 50 receipts per user per month

trajectory.detail: 10K users × 50 receipts/month = 500K receipts/month
Explicit capacity requirement — must be designed for from start
Calculated aggregate volume for architecture sizing

non_functional_requirements.title: GDPR Compliance
non_functional_requirements.description: The system shall comply with GDPR requirements including data minimisation, right to erasure, consent management, and data portability for UK and EU users

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated — 'must comply with GDPR as we're targeting UK and EU users'. Non-negotiable regulatory requirement.

citation.source_reference: must comply with GDPR as we're targeting UK and EU users

trajectory.detail: GDPR + UK/EU targeting explicitly stated
Regulatory — 'must comply' = Must-Have. Affects data storage, retention, deletion.
Expanded to key GDPR rights: erasure, minimisation, consent, portability

non_functional_requirements.title: MVP Launch Timeline
non_functional_requirements.description: The system MVP shall be delivered within 12 weeks from project start

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated — 'launch an MVP in 12 weeks'. Hard deadline constraint.

citation.source_reference: launch an MVP in 12 weeks

trajectory.detail: 12-week MVP timeline explicitly stated
Constrains scope — Must-Have features must fit in 12 weeks
Categorised as NFR-Performance (delivery performance)

metadata.reasoning: Fixed technology decision explicitly stated — 'building this on AWS (backend)'. Constrains service selection to AWS ecosystem.

citation.source_reference: building this on AWS (backend)

constraints.description: Mobile framework: React Native (cross-platform iOS and Android)

metadata.reasoning: Fixed technology decision — 'React Native (mobile app, cross-platform iOS and Android)'. Determines both platforms from single codebase.

citation.source_reference: React Native (mobile app, cross-platform iOS and Android)

constraints.description: Must comply with GDPR (UK and EU data protection)

metadata.reasoning: Regulatory constraint — non-negotiable legal requirement for UK/EU users.

citation.source_reference: must comply with GDPR as we're targeting UK and EU users

metadata.reasoning: Fixed timeline decision — constrains what can be included in first release.

citation.source_reference: launch an MVP in 12 weeks

constraints.description: Target audience: UK/EU small business owners (freelancers, sole traders, micro-businesses 1-5 employees)

metadata.reasoning: Fixed audience scope — affects localisation, tax compliance, terminology decisions.

citation.source_reference: freelancers, sole traders, and micro-businesses with 1-5 employees

assumptions.description: OCR extraction will require a third-party service (e.g., AWS Textract, Google Vision) rather than a custom-built model

metadata.reasoning: Input says 'using OCR' but doesn't specify build vs buy. Given 12-week MVP timeline, third-party service is strongly implied but not confirmed.

assumptions.description: The 7 predefined expense categories align with HMRC self-assessment categories for the tax summary feature

metadata.reasoning: Input lists predefined categories AND mentions HMRC tax summary separately. Unclear if they're the same set or if mapping is needed.

assumptions.description: Xero/QuickBooks integration is explicitly out of scope for MVP — standalone operation only

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated — 'integrate with Xero and QuickBooks...in a future phase, but the MVP should work standalone'. Confirmed by stakeholder.

assumptions.description: Push notifications require Apple Push Notification Service (APNs) and Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) integration

metadata.reasoning: React Native cross-platform + push notifications implies APNs + FCM, but no specific push service mentioned.

gaps.description: No data retention policy specified — how long are receipts and expense records stored?
gaps.impact: Cannot design storage architecture, backup strategy, or GDPR compliance for data lifecycle
gaps.suggested_question: How long should expense data be retained? Indefinitely? 7 years (UK tax records)? Should users be able to delete all their data (GDPR right to erasure)?

metadata.reasoning: GDPR requires defined retention periods. Tax records in UK must be kept 5-7 years. No guidance given — critical for architecture.

gaps.description: No multi-user/team functionality specified — can multiple employees in a micro-business share an account or view consolidated expenses?
gaps.impact: Cannot design data model for single-user vs multi-user. Affects permissions, consolidation, and reporting.
gaps.suggested_question: Is this single-user only, or can a business owner see all employees' expenses? Do employees submit for approval? Or is each user completely independent?

metadata.reasoning: Target audience includes 'micro-businesses with 1-5 employees' but all features are described in single-user terms. Unclear if multi-user is needed for MVP.

gaps.description: No currency specification — single currency (GBP) or multi-currency support?
gaps.impact: Cannot design amount storage, display formatting, or exchange rate handling. UK/EU targeting implies potential multi-currency need.
gaps.suggested_question: Should the app support only GBP, or also EUR and other currencies? If multi-currency, is exchange rate conversion needed for reporting?

metadata.reasoning: UK + EU targeting means users may have expenses in GBP and EUR. No mention of currency handling anywhere in the input.

gaps.description: No error recovery UX specified for failed OCR extraction
gaps.impact: Cannot design the user flow when OCR fails or produces low-confidence results. Affects user satisfaction.
gaps.suggested_question: When OCR fails or confidence is low, should the app: (a) show extracted data with red flags for review, (b) fall back to manual entry, or (c) queue for retry? What's the expected OCR accuracy threshold?

metadata.reasoning: OCR will not be 100% accurate. No guidance on the failure UX — critical for user experience with the core feature.

output.execution_summary:
• Produced 12 FRs (6 Must-Have, 4 Should-Have, 1 Could-Have, 1 Should-Have), 5 NFRs (4 Must-Have, 1 Should-Have), 5 constraints, 4 assumptions, 4 gaps
• Key decisions: classified offline/sync as NFR (not FR) — quality attribute. Separated custom categories from predefined (different priorities). Weekly summary demoted to Could-Have (engagement, not core).
• Reflection: initially missed FR-12 (biometric auth) — added. Initially had NFR-05 (12-week timeline) as constraint only — duplicated as NFR since it affects delivery performance. Verified all input paragraphs covered.
• 4 gaps need stakeholder input before architecture: data retention, multi-user model, currency support, OCR failure UX.
